title: Rule
description: Normalized, CSP-neutral rule entry in the catalog. Sources map into this shape; runtime never reads a source-specific format.

id.description: Globally unique, source-independent rule identity (basis for dedup).

version.description: Rule semver; a bad set is revertible by version.

resource_type.description: Normalized CSP-neutral type, e.g. compute.vm, database.postgres, network.public_ip.

reference.description: policies/ path or inline expression id.

check_logic.description: Deterministic predicate. T0 loads and evaluates this without engine changes.

cost_impact_monthly_usd.description: Estimated monthly cost impact. null → treated as unknown by the risk gate → HIL.

source_version.description: Human-readable upstream tag or release (e.g. v1.4.0); paired with `resolved_ref` for reproducibility.

resolved_ref.description: Immutable commit / digest the fetch pinned to (rename of the legacy `resolved_revision`; docs/roadmap/rule-catalog-collection.md § YAML Normalization).

license.description: SPDX identifier (e.g. `Apache-2.0`) or `LicenseRef-reference-only` for restricted sources.

redistribution.description: Redistribution posture - `embeddable` allows verbatim source text in this repo; `reference-only` allows only authored logic plus a citation.

retrieved_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp when the source was fetched (rename of the legacy `imported_at`).

mapped_by.description: Role or pipeline id that mapped the source into the normalized schema (never a person).

provenance.description: Auditable origin. Missing provenance = rule is rejected at load.

applies_to.description: Scope predicates (labels, tags, tenants). CSP-neutral vocabulary.

remediates.description: Ontology dispatch: the ActionType `name` this rule proposes on match (M:1). MUST resolve to a registered `rule-catalog/action-types/*.yaml` instance; the loader enforces cross-reference at load time. See docs/roadmap/llm-strategy.md § Rule as Ontology Artifact.

alternatives.description: Alternative ActionType names ranked by preference. T0 always uses `remediates`; only the T2 quality gate may swap in an alternative. Every entry MUST resolve to a registered ActionType.

parameters.description: Optional typed inputs to `check_logic` (e.g. retention thresholds, CVSS version). Defaults live on the rule; per-assignment overrides live in rule-governance.
